metadata.title: Estoril Praia Analytics Hub
metadata.description: Analytics dashboard for Estoril Praia SAD, with the history of Grupo Desportivo Estoril Praia.

home.recentResultsEmpty: No matches played yet this season.

charts.noMatchesYet: No matches played yet this season to chart.

home.noData: No synced data yet. Set `DATABASE_URL` and `FOOTBALL_DATA_API_KEY` and run the sync — see the "About the Data" page.

squad.title: Squad
squad.intro: Current players of Estoril Praia SAD, the club that competes in the Primeira Liga. Name, shirt number and position entered manually from the official site; age, nationality and market value cross-checked against ZeroZero.pt. Age may be off by up to a year (the source only lists age, not an exact date of birth), and not every player has a market value available — see the "About the Data" page.
squad.noData: The squad isn't available yet. The football-data.org free tier doesn't include this data for this competition — see the "About the Data" page for details. This page is ready for whenever that data exists, whether via the API or manual entry.

comparator.title: Player Comparator
comparator.intro: Compare 2025/26 season goals and assists between two players. Only players with confirmed stats are available — see "About the Data".

clubHistory.title: History
clubHistory.intro: Historical milestones of Grupo Desportivo Estoril Praia, since its founding in 1939. Manual dataset, cross-checked against the Portuguese and English Wikipedia — see the "About the Data" page.

founding.title: Founding
founding.description: Founded on 17 May 1939 as Grupo Desportivo Estoril Plage, by Fausto Cardoso de Figueiredo, out of the Sociedade Estoril Plage. The club's yellow and blue colours represent the region's sun and sea — the origin of the "Canarinhos" ("Canaries") nickname.

firstSport1940.title: First sport
firstSport1940.description: Open-water swimming at Praia do Tamariz was the club's first sport. The football section was founded that same year.

inauguralParade1940.title: Inaugural sports parade
inauguralParade1940.description: First sports parade at the Campo de Jogos, attended by President Óscar Carmona and Education Minister Mário de Figueiredo.

headquarters1941.title: New social headquarters
headquarters1941.description: New social headquarters inaugurated at Palácio Castanheira, on the club's second anniversary, with President Óscar Carmona in attendance.

cupFinal1944.title: First promotion to the top flight
cupFinal1944.description: Earned its first-ever promotion to Portugal's top football division, in a season that also saw the club reach the Taça de Portugal final, lost 8–0 to Benfica.

debut1945.title: Top-flight debut
debut1945.description: Debut season in the top division, finishing 7th.

bestEarlyFinish1948.title: Best finish of the decade
bestEarlyFinish1948.description: 4th place in the top division — a result that would only be matched 66 years later, in 2013/14.

goldenScorer1949.title: Miguel Lourenço's finest season
goldenScorer1949.description: One of the club's most prolific forwards (139 appearances for Estoril) hit his career-best record: 19 goals in just 21 games this season.

recordWin1950.title: Biggest-ever win
recordWin1950.description: A historic 10–0 win over Lusitano Sport Clube.

relegation1953.title: Relegated from the top flight
relegation1953.description: Relegated from the top division, beginning more than two decades in the regional divisions.

stadiumRenamed1972.title: Estádio António Coimbra da Mota
stadiumRenamed1972.description: The stadium was renamed "Estádio António Coimbra da Mota" on 13 December, honouring the club's late vice-president.

return1976.title: Return to the top flight
return1976.description: Returned to the top division after more than 20 years in the lower tiers, with five consecutive seasons featuring players like António Simões and Fernando Santos.

relegation1980.title: Relegated again
relegation1980.description: Relegated to the second division after the cycle that began in 1975/76.

promotion1981.title: Immediate promotion
promotion1981.description: Promoted straight back to the top division, playing three consecutive seasons through to 1983/84.

relegation1984.title: Relegated to the second division
relegation1984.description: Relegated after three seasons in the top flight.

promotion1991.title: Return to the top flight
promotion1991.description: Promoted to the top division as runners-up of the Segunda Divisão de Honra, playing three consecutive seasons through to 1993/94.

relegation1994.title: Relegated to the second division
relegation1994.description: Relegated after the cycle that began in 1990/91.

pauleta1996.title: Pauleta's launchpad
pauleta1996.description: At 22, Pauleta scored around 19 goals for Estoril in the Second Division under coach Carlos Manuel. The season caught the attention of Spanish clubs and launched the career of the future all-time top scorer for the Portuguese national team.

title2003.title: Segunda Divisão B champions
title2003.description: Won the Segunda Divisão B title under Ulisses Morais, the first of two consecutive promotions.

title2004.title: Liga de Honra champions
title2004.description: Won the Liga de Honra (second division) title, earning promotion to the Primeira Liga — the second consecutive promotion, also under Ulisses Morais.

relegation2005.title: Relegated again
relegation2005.description: Relegated to the second division after a single season back in the Primeira Liga.

financialCrisis2006.title: Financial crisis
financialCrisis2006.description: A financial crisis required a rescue by João Lagos's Grupo Lagos Sports.

trafficSports2010.title: New ownership
trafficSports2010.description: Financial and sporting stabilization under the management of Traffic Sports Europe.

title2012.title: Marco Silva arrives and promotes Estoril
title2012.description: Marco Silva, appointed director of football in June 2011, takes over as manager midway through the season with the team 10th in the Liga de Honra. He loses just 3 of the next 24 games and wins the title, returning Estoril to the Primeira Liga 7 years after the 2004/05 relegation. He's named the competition's Coach of the Year.

european2013.title: 5th place and first European qualification
european2013.description: Now in the Primeira Liga, Marco Silva stays on as manager and leads Estoril to 5th place, securing the club's first-ever qualification for European competition.

bestFinish2014.title: 4th place — best-ever finish
bestFinish2014.description: Under Marco Silva, Estoril finish 4th in the Primeira Liga, matching the 1947/48 record as the club's best-ever finish, and make their debut in European competition (Europa League). At season's end, Marco Silva announces his departure for new challenges.

anniversary2014.title: 75th anniversary
anniversary2014.description: Celebrated the club's 75th anniversary, with the local musical group Ténis Bar creating the official anthem "És Estoril".

newHeadquarters2017.title: New sustainable headquarters
newHeadquarters2017.description: A new sustainable social headquarters was inaugurated on 11 January, with a museum space dedicated to the club's history.

title2021.title: Liga Portugal 2 champions
title2021.description: Won the Liga Portugal 2 title, earning a return to the Primeira Liga.

sub23Success.title: Sub-23 success
sub23Success.description: The Sub-23 team won three Liga Revelação titles in five seasons, plus one Taça Revelação.

cupRunnerUp2024.title: Taça da Liga runners-up
cupRunnerUp2024.description: Runners-up in the Taça da Liga, lost on penalties.

news.title: News
news.intro: Latest news from Estoril Praia SAD, entered manually from the official site.

aboutData.title: About the Data
aboutData.intro: This project was built as a portfolio piece and combines real data — fetched via API and entered manually — with experimental sections that are clearly labelled whenever the data is simulated.
aboutData.entityTitle: Estoril Praia SAD vs. Grupo Desportivo Estoril Praia
aboutData.entityNote: These are different entities. Estoril Praia SAD runs the first team (Primeira Liga) and the Sub-23 side — it's what the standings, fixtures, squad, news and stats on this site refer to. Grupo Desportivo Estoril Praia is the parent club, responsible for the youth academy and other sports sections — it's only referenced on the "History" page, about the club's 1939 founding. football-data.org returns "GD Estoril Praia" as the team name since that's the club's popular name; this site corrects the display to "Estoril Praia SAD" in the contexts where that's the accurate entity, without altering the synced data itself.

sources.standings: Standings, results and fixtures — football-data.org (free tier), via REST API.
sources.squad: Squad — name, number and position entered manually from the official site (football-data.org doesn't provide the full squad for Liga Portugal); age, nationality and market value cross-checked against ZeroZero.pt.
sources.history: Club history — manual dataset, built from public sources (Wikipedia, official site).
sources.news: Club news — periodic manual entry, from the official Estoril Praia website.
sources.marketValues: Market values — occasional manual entry from ZeroZero.pt (Sofascore and FBref were considered first, but Sofascore loads values via JavaScript and FBref doesn't publish market values; never Transfermarkt, due to its terms of use). Only 14 of 23 players have a value available in the source.
sources.playerStats: Goals and assists for the 2025/26 season (for the top scorers chart and the comparator) — manual dataset, cross-checked across ESPN, Soccerway and FootyStats (FBref, the spec's preferred source, blocks automated access). Only 8 of 23 players have a confirmed stat.

aboutData.updateFrequency: Standings, results and fixtures are synced by a scheduled task (Vercel Cron Job) that runs once a day and writes the results into the project's own database. The site never calls the external API directly on each visit, to respect the free tier's rate limits. The squad, club history and news are entered manually and updated occasionally.
aboutData.limitations: The football-data.org free tier has request-per-minute limits, doesn't include the full squad for Liga Portugal (so the squad is entered manually), and may not include every match event (goals, cards, substitutions) for every competition. Between seasons, the API pairs the previous season's final standings with information about the next one — the site detects this and labels the standings with the season the data actually belongs to (e.g. "2025/26"), so it's never mistaken for the upcoming season's table. Player age is approximate (based only on the age listed by ZeroZero.pt, not an exact date of birth, so it may be off by up to a year). Not every player has a market value available in the source. Some historical or financial data is only updated manually and occasionally.
aboutData.simulatedData: Any artificially generated advanced metric (e.g. fatigue, GPS, wearables) is clearly flagged with a "Simulated data" notice in the interface and is never presented as real.

password.errorTooShort: The new password must be at least 8 characters long.
